User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user account in the OmniVid AI application, linked to an external Google authentication system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

externalAuthId.description: Identifier from the external authentication system (e.g., Google's user ID) used to link the app user to their external account. This is not a credential.

email.description: User's email address, obtained from the external authentication provider.

displayName.description: User's display name, obtained from the external authentication provider.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile image, obtained from the external authentication provider.

preferredLanguage.description: User's preferred language for the application interface and default prompt input (e.g., 'en', 'es', 'hi').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the user account details were updated.

Video.title: Video
Video.description: Represents a video generated by the OmniVid AI application from user prompts.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Video entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who generated this video. (Relationship: User 1:N Video)

originalPrompt.description: The original text prompt provided by the user in their native language.

optimizedPrompt.description: The AI-optimized English version of the prompt used for video generation.

targetVoiceoverLanguage.description: The target language and dialect for the video's AI-generated voiceover (e.g., 'en-US', 'hi-IN').

outputVideoUrl.description: URL to the final generated video file, stored in Google Cloud Storage.

thumbnailImageUrl.description: URL to a thumbnail image for the generated video.

status.description: Current status of the video generation process (e.g., 'PENDING', 'GENERATING', 'COMPLETED', 'FAILED', 'SAFETY_BLOCKED').

resolution.description: The resolution of the generated video (e.g., '720p', '1080p').

queueType.description: The type of generation queue used for this video ('STANDARD' for longer wait, 'FAST_PASS' for ad-supported quicker generation).

durationSeconds.description: The duration of the generated video in seconds.

watermarked.description: Indicates whether the generated video contains an invisible digital watermark.

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the video generation request was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating the last time the video's status or details were updated.

generationStartedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the video generation process officially started.

generationCompletedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the video generation process was completed.

safetyScanStatus.description: The result of the content safety scan for the generated video ('SCAN_PENDING', 'SAFE', 'FLAGGED', 'BLOCKED').

safetyScanDetails.description: Optional details or reasons if the video was flagged or blocked by the safety scanner.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The document ID (`{userId}`) must match the Firebase Authentication UID (`request.auth.uid`). The document also contains `id` and `externalAuthId` fields which will mirror the document ID for explicit ownership and integration with external auth.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Stores videos generated by a specific user. Each video document includes a denormalized 'userId' field that explicitly links it to the owner, enabling Authorization Independence for security rules and supporting QAPs. The '{userId}' parameter in the path must match the 'userId' field within the document and the authenticated user's UID.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns this video, matching their Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: videoId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific generated video.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for OmniVid AI prioritizes Authorization Independence and QAPs (Queryable Access Patterns) by leveraging a combination of path-based ownership and strategic denormalization. 

1.  **Users Collection (`/users/{userId}`):** User profiles are stored in a top-level collection. Each user's document ID (`{userId}`) is directly mapped to their Firebase Authentication UID (`request.auth.uid`). This establishes clear, path-based ownership for individual user data, making rules simple: `allow read, update, delete: if request.auth.uid == userId;` and `allow create: if request.auth.uid == userId && exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/users/$(userId)) == false;`.

2.  **Videos Subcollection (`/users/{userId}/videos/{videoId}`):** Each user's generated videos are stored as a subcollection under their respective user document. This hierarchical structure inherently links videos to their owners. 

**Authorization Independence (Denormalization):**
Crucially, every `Video` document includes a `userId` field within its data. This `userId` field stores the ID of the owning user, which is also present in the document's path (`/users/{userId}/videos/{videoId}`). This denormalization means that Firestore Security Rules can check ownership directly within the `Video` document itself (`resource.data.userId == request.auth.uid`) without needing to perform expensive or transaction-breaking `get()` operations on the parent `/users/{userId}` document. This ensures that creating, updating, or deleting a video is an atomic operation from a security rule perspective, enhancing performance and debuggability.

**QAPs (Queryable Access Patterns):**
This structure strongly supports QAPs for listing a user's videos. When a user requests to view their videos, the application can issue a query scoped directly to their personal subcollection: `firestore.collection('users').doc(request.auth.uid).collection('videos')`. The security rules can then easily enforce `allow list: if request.auth.uid == userId;` (where `userId` is the wildcard from the path). This ensures that a user can only list videos from their own designated subcollection, preventing unauthorized access to other users' video lists. The `userId` field within the `Video` document also allows for efficient indexed queries if more complex filtering within a user's videos is ever needed, e.g., `where('status', '==', 'COMPLETED')`.
